name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for this platform instance

host.title: Processor Host
host.description: IP address or hostname of the StormAudio ISP processor

port.title: Port
port.description: TCP port for the StormAudio ISP API (default: 23)

volumeFloor.title: Volume Floor (dB)
volumeFloor.description: Minimum volume level in dB, must be less than volumeCeiling (default: -100)

volumeControl.title: Volume Control
volumeControl.description: HomeKit accessory type used for volume control. Fan is recommended — it avoids 'turn off all lights' muting your processor.

wakeTimeout.title: Wake Timeout (seconds)
wakeTimeout.description: How long to wait for the processor to boot after a power-on command (default: 90). Older models may need a higher value.

commandInterval.title: Command Interval (ms)
commandInterval.description: Minimum milliseconds between commands sent to the processor (default: 100). Values below 85 may cause dropped commands.

inputs.title: Input Name Aliases
inputs.description: Override input names from the processor for Siri-friendly naming. Keys are input ID numbers (visible in the Homebridge log on startup), values are display names. Example: { "3": "Apple TV", "5": "PS5" }

zoneId.title: Zone 2 Zone ID
zoneId.description: Zone ID from your StormAudio processor (set via the dropdown at the top of this page, or enter manually)

name.title: Zone 2 Name
name.description: Display name for the Zone 2 accessory in HomeKit (e.g., 'Patio')

volumeControl.title: Zone 2 Volume Control
volumeControl.description: Volume proxy for Zone 2. Default is 'none' — volume control is available via the TV tile's remote buttons.

volumeFloor.title: Zone 2 Volume Floor (dB)
volumeFloor.description: Minimum dB for Zone 2 volume mapping (maps to 0%). Default: -80

volumeCeiling.title: Zone 2 Volume Ceiling (dB)
volumeCeiling.description: Maximum dB for Zone 2 volume mapping (maps to 100%). Default: -20

enabled.title: Enable Presets Accessory
enabled.description: Create a separate HomeKit accessory for theater preset selection. Presets are imported from the processor at connection time.

name.title: Preset Accessory Name
name.description: Display name for the preset accessory in HomeKit (e.g., 'Theater Presets')

aliases.title: Preset Name Aliases
aliases.description: Override preset names from the processor. Keys are preset IDs (as strings), values are display names. Example: { "9": "Movie Night", "12": "Music" }

triggers.title: Triggers
triggers.description: Configure hardware triggers (1-4) as HomeKit accessories. Each trigger can be a Switch (bidirectional control) or Contact Sensor (read-only state).

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for this trigger in HomeKit

type.title: Type
type.description: How this trigger appears in HomeKit. Switch allows control; Contact Sensor is read-only but usable as an automation trigger.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for this trigger in HomeKit

type.title: Type
type.description: How this trigger appears in HomeKit. Switch allows control; Contact Sensor is read-only but usable as an automation trigger.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for this trigger in HomeKit

type.title: Type
type.description: How this trigger appears in HomeKit. Switch allows control; Contact Sensor is read-only but usable as an automation trigger.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for this trigger in HomeKit

type.title: Type
type.description: How this trigger appears in HomeKit. Switch allows control; Contact Sensor is read-only but usable as an automation trigger.
